title: Control Totals Validation
text:
0.1 What it validates
This dashboard summarizes and validates the output of the control_totals pipeline. Each chapter is a self-contained Jupyter notebook in summary_scripts/.

title: Control Totals Validation
text:
Output vs. input targets — do the rebased control totals reach the county / RG growth targets at the target year?
Output vs. legacy LUVit — how do the new control totals compare to results from the legacy R pipeline (examples/legacy_luvit/output)?
Internal consistency — control-area sums add to county and regional totals, no negative values, HCT and non-HCT splits balance.

title: Control Totals Validation
section: 0.2 Pointing the dashboard at a run
text:
0.2 Pointing the dashboard at a run
The file config.yaml controls which run output is summarized:
example_path: ../../examples/summer_2026   # required
legacy_path:  ../../examples/legacy_luvit  # optional comparison
Paths are relative to the validation/ folder. Edit config.yaml, re-execute the notebooks, then re-render the book.

title: Control Totals Validation
text:
0.3 Rendering the book
Install Quarto and from the control_totals/validation/ folder run:
quarto render
Output is written to validation-dashboard/. Notebooks are not executed on render (execute.enabled: false); they ship with their committed outputs. To re-execute everything before rendering:
jupyter nbconvert --to notebook --execute summary_scripts/*.ipynb --inplace
quarto render
To render just one notebook (using its committed output):
quarto render summary_scripts/population.ipynb

title: Control Totals Validation
text:
0.4 Adding a new chapter

Drop a new .ipynb in summary_scripts/.
In any cell, import the shared helpers:
from validation_data_input import load_controls, load_targets, load_settings
from util import apply_plotly_theme, COUNTY_NAME
Execute the notebook (so its outputs are committed).
Add the path to the chapters: list in _quarto.yml.
quarto render.

title: Control Totals Validation
text:
0.5 Layout reference
validation/
├── _quarto.yml              book config + sidebar
├── index.qmd                this page
├── config.yaml              which run to validate
├── styling/custom.scss      light/dark theme tweaks
├── PSRClogo2016path.png     PSRC logo (sidebar)
└── summary_scripts/
    ├── util.py              plotly + pandas helpers
    ├── validation_data_input.py   cached data loaders
    └── *.ipynb              one chapter per notebook

title: 2  Population
text:
2.4 Notes

Base year, targets year, and end year come from configs/settings.yaml of the active example.
Legacy comparison loads examples/legacy_luvit/output/total_pop_estimates_all_years.csv if present and non-empty.

title: 3  Housing units
text:
3.4 Notes

HHGro2350 is the input target growth (base_year → targets_end_year) from TargetsRebasedOutput.xlsx → CityHH.
A small percent difference is expected from rounding and from any RG-level scaling applied during interpolation.

title: 4  Employment
text:
4.4 Notes

Sector breakdown (e.g. resource/construction split applied in emp_chg_targets_*.py) is not in the final controls workbook; to validate sector totals, add a new notebook that reads from the intermediate HDF5 outputs.

title: 5  Consistency checks
text:
5.1 Adding a new check
Internal QC checks on the produced control totals. Each row of the table is a single rule; failures highlight where to dig in.
Append a dict with check, pass, and detail keys to the checks list. Anything boolean works — combine pandas filters with .all() / .sum() for quick rules.
